name: Quick Turnip Pickles (Kabu No Sokuseki-zuke)
recipeIngredient: 1 square yuzu, citron, or lemon (2.5 cm)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and peel the turnips, then cut into very fine julienne strips.
2. Reserve the greens from the turnips.
3. Wash the greens, dry them, and chop finely.
4. Combine the turnip strips with the chopped greens in a bowl and sprinkle with salt.
5. Knead the mixture with your hands to evenly distribute the salt and draw out liquid.
6. Discard any excess liquid that forms.
7. Add the kelp pieces and the yuzu, citron, or lemon square to the bowl.
8. Cover the bowl loosely with a lid and let it stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
9. To serve, use chopsticks or a fork to remove a portion of the pickles from the bowl and shake off excess liquid.
10. Arrange the pickled turnips in individual serving dishes as desired. You may add a few drops of soy sauce if preferred.
This recipe is based on "Japanese Cooking: A Simple Art" by Shizuo Tsuji, with an introduction by M.F.K. Fischer (1980).
